name: native-module-bridge-turbo
description: Validate that the skill produces a correct Turbo Module implementation with TypeScript codegen spec, native platform implementations, and proper JSI integration following the new architecture patterns.
input: Build a Turbo Module for a custom biometric authentication feature in React Native. Create the TypeScript codegen spec with methods for checking biometric availability, authenticating with biometrics, and retrieving supported biometric types. Implement the native module for both iOS (Swift/Objective-C++) and Android (Kotlin). Use the new architecture with JSI for synchronous calls. Include proper error handling with typed error codes.

assertions.description: Must use the TurboModule interface for the native module specification, as this is the new architecture replacement for the legacy Bridge-based NativeModule.
Must use React Native's codegen system (codegenNativeComponent or codegenNativeCommands) to generate type-safe native bindings from the TypeScript spec.
Must retrieve the native module using TurboModuleRegistry.getEnforcing to ensure the module is available and throw early if registration failed.
Biometric authentication methods must return Promises for async operations, as biometric prompts require user interaction and OS-level dialog presentation.
Android implementation must extend ReactContextBaseJavaModule or implement TurboModule interface for proper lifecycle management and React Native integration.

name: performance-optimization
description: Validate that the skill produces a comprehensive performance optimization plan with profiling steps, specific optimizations for list rendering, and measurable performance targets.
input: Our React Native app has a product catalog screen with 2000+ items that drops to 15 FPS during fast scroll and takes 3 seconds for initial render. Profile and optimize this screen. The current implementation uses FlatList with complex item components that include images, price formatting, and rating stars. Each item has an onPress handler. The screen re-renders entirely when the search filter changes. Target 60 FPS scroll and under 500ms initial render.

assertions.description: Must recommend replacing FlatList with FlashList for large lists, as FlashList uses cell recycling to dramatically reduce memory allocation and improve scroll FPS.
Must wrap list item components with React.memo to prevent re-rendering unchanged items when the parent list re-renders due to data or filter changes.
Must stabilize onPress and other callback props with useCallback to prevent them from breaking React.memo's shallow comparison on every parent render.
Must configure FlashList's estimatedItemSize and getItemType for heterogeneous lists to enable accurate cell recycling and layout estimation.
Must verify or recommend Hermes as the JavaScript engine for faster startup via bytecode precompilation and improved garbage collection during scroll.
